seasonArc: Giulio Pellizzari's 2026 read as the year Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe's quiet investment in the 22-year-old Italian climber began to look obvious. Tirreno-Adriatico was the week he proved it across seven stages: third on Stage 2's wet gravel (same time as Van der Poel and Del Toro), second on Stage 4 with Pellizzari in the reduced front group, then losing 19 seconds to Del Toro's Stage 5 acceleration, finally fourth at +9 on the queen stage at Camerino. Not a single stage win, but a top-five GC at week's end alongside three Italian podium-tier stage finishes — the season's clearest signal that the country's next stage-race talent has arrived. He'll race the Giro in May (his home Grand Tour) and Romandie shaped his Italian profile further into the spring.
